chapter: ANATOMY AND FUNCTION OF UROGENITAL TRACT

differentials.name: ADULTHOOD liver ≥ spleen ≥ renal cortex > renal medulla
differentials.raw: ADULTHOOD liver ≥ spleen ≥ renal cortex > renal medulla

differentials.name: INFANCY
differentials.raw: INFANCY (in neonate up to 6 months of age)

differentials.name: LUMBAR VEINS JOINING LEFT RENAL VEIN
differentials.raw: LUMBAR VEINS JOINING LEFT RENAL VEIN (75%)

ADULTHOOD liver ≥ spleen ≥ renal cortex: cortex may be more echogenic than adjacent normal liver / spleen Cause:   glomeruli occupy 18% of cortex in neonate compared with 9% in adult
increase in corticomedullary differentiation Cause:   ratio of cortex to medulla 1.64÷1 in neonate compared with 2.59÷1 in adult
hypoechoic pyramids appear relatively large Cause:   thin immature cortex in neonate N.B.:   may be misinterpreted as dilated calices / renal cystic disease
renal sinus echogenicity less prominent Cause:   paucity of fat 1st order:   main renal arteries at level of L1 / upper margin of L2 2nd order:   5 segmental br
